title: Axeyum SMT-LIB ordered session contract prototype

prototype_policy.reset_success_uses: pre-command print-success and regular-output-channel, then enters a new default epoch
prototype_policy.set_option_output_uses: the newly assigned output option applies to the set-option response itself
prototype_policy.query_payloads: query-N placeholders identify the exact most-recent check snapshot; production payloads must be typed and replay-checked

invariants.rule: Every input command is interpreted in source order and every response is bound to that exact command point; final global maps may not answer earlier requests.
invariants.implementation_gate: One SessionEvent per processed command, with a monotonic command index and no output-query side vectors.

invariants.rule: The session has start, assert, sat, unsat, and terminated modes; commands outside the permitted mode return an error.
invariants.implementation_gate: Mode transition tests cover every command class from every mode, including unknown entering sat mode and exit from all modes.

invariants.rule: Under the selected continued-execution policy, an error emits one regular-channel error response and leaves all semantic state unchanged.
invariants.implementation_gate: Parse, sort, scope, option, and execution errors are staged before commit; mutation tests compare complete before/after state.

invariants.rule: Model/proof/core/value responses refer to the most recent check command and its exact context, assumptions, model, verdict, and evidence until invalidated or replaced.
invariants.implementation_gate: A QuerySnapshot carries epoch, query id, assertion generation, assumptions, result, model, evidence, and route; output commands never re-solve a reconstructed final script.

invariants.rule: Each produce-* option is false by default and disabled output commands return an error rather than data or a silent no-op.
invariants.implementation_gate: Negative transcript fixtures cover every produce-* gate in both the right and wrong query modes.

invariants.rule: print-success and output-channel changes apply to the response of the set-option command that changes them.
invariants.implementation_gate: The response event is constructed after option validation/commit and carries an output route snapshot, not a live writer reference.

invariants.rule: Declarations and definitions belong to the current assertion level by default and disappear on pop; global-declarations=true makes subsequent declarations permanent.
invariants.implementation_gate: Scope frames own assertions, declarations, definitions, aliases, and named terms; use-after-pop is an atomic error.

invariants.rule: reset-assertions clears all assertions and levels; it also removes non-global declarations/definitions while preserving options, set-logic declarations, and explicitly global declarations.
invariants.implementation_gate: Default/global declaration transcript pairs prove opposite visibility after reset-assertions without changing options.

invariants.rule: reset returns to startup state with default options and a fresh declaration/arena epoch; no TermId, model, or proof may cross the epoch boundary.
invariants.implementation_gate: SessionState swaps in a fresh epoch atomically and renders any reset success response from a pre-reset response snapshot.

invariants.rule: check-sat-assuming does not mutate the context but its assumptions belong to the resulting query snapshot.
invariants.implementation_gate: The next assertion/check sees the original context; unsat-assumption and proof eligibility read the snapshot, not the current stack.

invariants.rule: get-proof is legal only after an unsat check with an empty assumption set; unsat assumptions and cores remain distinct responses.
invariants.implementation_gate: Proof dispatch rejects a nonempty QuerySnapshot.assumptions before invoking any emitter.

invariants.rule: Specific responses are emitted regardless of print-success; success is only the optional response for commands without a specific result, and exit emits success before termination when enabled.
invariants.implementation_gate: SessionResponse is typed independently from rendering; the CLI routes a frozen regular/diagnostic channel tag after the pure state transition.

invariants.rule: Epoch, command, query, and assertion-generation identifiers are monotonic deterministic values and never derive from addresses, hash iteration, Debug text, or backend handles.
invariants.implementation_gate: Two runs produce byte-identical typed event sequences and transcripts; reset changes only the specified epoch-relative identities.

invariants.rule: The session core returns typed responses and output-route snapshots; filesystem/process I/O belongs to an adapter so library, CLI, WASM, and tests share semantics.
invariants.implementation_gate: No std::fs write or process exit occurs below the adapter; an in-memory transcript sink exercises the complete contract.

implementation_findings.fact: ScriptCommand records assertion-stack operations and get-assertions, not the complete protocol stream.
implementation_findings.consequence: Output/lifecycle commands cannot be associated with their exact state or preceding query.

implementation_findings.fact: Option updates collapse into one final map while get-option requests live in a separate list.
implementation_findings.consequence: A request can observe a later value instead of the value at its command point.

contains: Output/query commands: accepted as no-ops at parse time
implementation_findings.fact: Several standard output commands are syntax-only no-ops.
implementation_findings.consequence: Parser acceptance currently overstates executable command conformance.

implementation_findings.fact: Full reset is rejected because every parsed term shares one arena.
implementation_findings.consequence: Production reset requires a fresh arena/session epoch, not clearing vectors in place.

contains: mutate the shared arena directly (and stay global)
implementation_findings.fact: Declarations stay global across push/pop regardless of the standard global-declarations default.
implementation_findings.consequence: The current incremental helper is assertion-stack equivalent, not protocol/signature equivalent.

implementation_findings.fact: Pop consumes only available scopes and silently ignores underflow.
implementation_findings.consequence: A malformed command changes neither output nor error state as SMT-LIB requires; the runner needs transactional validation.

implementation_findings.fact: No public session state or typed response contract exists.
implementation_findings.consequence: The new boundary can be designed without preserving an accidental public session ABI.

implementation_findings.fact: CheckResult carries a model only for Sat, while SMT-LIB places both sat and unknown in its sat query mode.
implementation_findings.consequence: The session needs an explicit deterministic model-inspection policy for unknown; mode legality alone cannot manufacture a replayed model.

fixtures.purpose: The print-success update affects its own response; disabling it suppresses its own success while specific echo output remains.
Changing the regular output channel routes the set-option success response and subsequent output to the new channel.
get-option observes state at its own command point rather than the final option map.
With the standard default, a declaration made after push disappears on pop and use-after-pop is an atomic error.
global-declarations makes subsequent declarations survive pop.
reset-assertions removes non-global declarations as well as assertions and levels.
reset-assertions preserves declarations made global by the start-mode option.
Full reset returns to start defaults while its optional success response uses the pre-reset output snapshot.
Pop underflow is an error and continued execution sees the unchanged assertion stack.
A sat result does not bypass the default-disabled produce-models option, and the error preserves sat mode.
Values and models bind to the exact preceding sat query while a later unsat query replaces the prior snapshot.
A later unsat check invalidates model inspection and enables proof inspection for only the new query.
Proof retrieval rejects an unsat check with assumptions while get-unsat-assumptions reads that same snapshot.
A start-only produce option cannot be enabled after set-logic, and the failed update does not leak through a later model request.
The standard sat query mode includes unknown; enabled model inspection remains mode-legal even if production may return a partial value.
Exit is valid in every mode, emits success first when enabled, and suppresses all later commands.
An unsupported command emits a specific response without mutating the session, which then continues.
get-assertions is option-gated and observes assertion depth at each exact request.
An enabled unsat core is tied to the exact preceding unsat query.
Duplicate declarations fail without removing the original visible symbol.
